steps.title: Recognize the passage structure
steps.content:
The passage opens with a definition of “loanword,” then demonstrates this concept through two examples: “coyote” from an Indigenous language (Nahuatl) and “condor” from another Indigenous language (Quechua).

The strongest clue is that the passage is specifically organized to show examples of English words borrowed from Indigenous languages.

steps.title: Identify the main idea
steps.content:
Choice A (‘The English words "coyote" and "condor" are both examples of loanwords from Indigenous languages.’) states exactly what the passage demonstrates: “coyote” and “condor” are both loanwords from Indigenous languages.

The other choices, and
  • D each go beyond or contradict the passage—B makes an unsupported generalization
  • C flips the direction
  • D introduces a meaning change the text doesn't address


That confirms A is correct.
